dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9665 IH: VA Police Security Enhancement Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Van Orden introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Veterans' Affairs

form.official-title: To amend title 38, United States Code, to expand the jurisdiction of Department of Veterans Affairs police officers, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the VA Police Security Enhancement Act.

section.header: Department of Veterans Affairs Police Security Enhancement

subsection.text: Section 901 of title 38, United States Code, is amended, in subsection (a)(2), by adding at the end the following:

list.list-item: (A) land and buildings that are occupied by or under the custody or control of the Department;
(B) land and buildings leased by or on behalf of the Department;
(C) land and buildings leased by the Department to another entity, including property subject to an enhanced use lease agreement; and
(D) the area immediately surrounding such land or buildings.

subsection.text: Such section is further amended by adding at the end the following new subsection:

The Secretary shall seek to enter into memoranda of understanding with the appropriate local, State, and Federal law enforcement agencies to facilitate the exercise of authority by Department police officers on Department property under the jurisdiction of such agencies.
Section 902(a) of such title is amended—

subparagraph.text: in subparagraph (F), by striking (E) and inserting (G);
by redesignating subparagraphs (C) through (F) as subparagraphs (E) through (H), respectively; and
by inserting after clause (B) the following new clauses:
Protect veterans, officials and employees of the Department, other individuals, and Department property;
Ensure the physical security and operational continuity of facilities of the Department;
Carry out other such security functions as are expressly authorized by statute;

paragraph.text: by adding at the end the following new paragraphs:
The authorities under paragraph (1) may be exercised off Department property only when—

subparagraph.text: necessary to protect officials or employees of the Department and Department property;
pursuant to a written agreement under section 901(g) of this title.

subsection.text: Such section is further amended by adding at the end the following new subsections:
The Secretary shall implement measures to enhance security at facilities of the Department, including—

paragraph.text: coordination with other law enforcement agencies.
Upon request of the Committee on Veterans’ Affairs of the House of Representatives or the Senate, the Secretary, without delay or nonstatutory conditions, shall provide—

subparagraph.text: policies and directives under this chapter;
data on arrests and use of force by Department police officers; and
briefings regarding the operations of Department police officers.

paragraph.text: The Secretary shall submit to Congress an annual report including—

subparagraph.text: a description of staffing levels of Department police officers;
an identification of capability gaps among Department police officers;
a description of the training of Department police officers; and
a description of enforcement activity of Department police officers in the year preceding the report.

paragraph.text: Chapter 9 of such title is amended by adding at the end the following new section:

section.header: Veterans Affairs Police and Security Operations Account

subsection.text: There is hereby established in the Treasury a separate account to be known as the Veterans Affairs Police and Security Operations Account.
There shall be deposited in the account such amounts as may be appropriated pursuant to subsection (c).
There is authorized to be appropriated to the account amounts for the following purposes:

paragraph.text: Employment and training of Department police officers.

subsection.text: This section shall cease to have effect on the date that is three years after the date of the enactment of this section.

paragraph.text: The table of contents at the beginning of such chapter is amended by adding at the end the following new item:

toc.toc-entry: 906. Veterans Affairs Police and Security Operations Account.

subsection.text: Not later than 180 days after the enactment of this Act, the Secretary shall prescribe regulations to enforce the amendments made by this Act.

Not later than two years after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Comptroller General of the United States shall submit to the Committees on Veterans’ Affairs of the House of Representatives and the Senate a report evaluating—

paragraph.text: the effectiveness of the authorities provided under sections 901 and 902 of title 38, United States Code;
coordination with Federal, State, and local law enforcement;
the adequacy of the training and operational standards of Department of Veterans Affairs police officers; and
recommendations for the continuation, modification, or termination of the authorities provided under such sections.
